name: OSM features by named place
when_to_use: Count or list OSM POIs, buildings, pedestrian streets, green spaces, amenities, or other tagged features inside a named city, district, or neighborhood.
verified_snippet:
import osmnx as ox

ox.settings.use_cache = True
ox.settings.requests_timeout = 60
place = '{place}'
tags = {'amenity': True}
gdf = ox.features_from_place(place, tags=tags)
print(f'features_count={len(gdf)}')
cols = [c for c in ['name', 'amenity'] if c in gdf.columns]
if cols and len(gdf):
    print(gdf[cols].drop_duplicates().head(20).to_string(index=False))
io_contract: Input: named place string and OSM tags dict. Output: count and optional table of matching features. Use printed values as measured OSM observations.
pitfalls: OSM coverage varies by place; absence in OSM is not proof of real absence.
Large places can be slow; narrow tags or switch to features_from_point with dist.
